text: REST APIs are a common way for applications to communicate over the internet. When designing such an API, it's important to follow best practices. First, use clear and logical endpoints, such as `/users` to access user data. Status codes like 200 for success or 404 for not found help clients understand the result of their requests. Always provide thorough documentation so developers can easily integrate with your API. Finally, use versioning, for example by adding `/v1/` to your endpoints, to ensure changes don't break existing integrations.
